figures.caption: Table 7: Intended model inference parameters. GPT-5-nano and GPT-5-mini use reasoning_effort and are pinned to the 2025-08-07 snapshot; GPT-OSS-120B has no versioned snapshot and uses temperature and max_tokens.
Figure 3: System and user prompt templates used for LLM-as-a-judge evaluation.
Table 4: Cumulative cost per user at context length 101,601 tokens as a function of N interaction turns. LC prices assume prompt caching from turn 2 onward (90% discount on input tokens).
Figure 2: Custom instructions passed to the Mem0 add stage for memory extraction.

Figure 1: Break-even heatmap: cumulative cost difference (LC minus Memory) as a function of context length L and number of turns N . Red regions indicate where the long-context approach is cheaper; blue regions indicate where the memory system is cheaper. The black curve marks the break-even boundary.

Table 6: Concrete examples of information categories irretrievably lost under flat-typed fact extraction (LongMemEval). “LC answer” refers to LC GPT-5-mini, which reads the full conversation and answers correctly in all three cases.
